title: fragrant, aerated mexican pork tacos
description: yucatecan-style pork tacos with a fragrant and aerated twist, featuring mustard seeds and a nimono-simmer
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tbsp of oil over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mustard seeds, coriander seeds, cumin, and chili powder. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the pork shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the lime juice and brown sugar. Stir to combine.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and braise the pork for 2.5 hours, or until tender.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it rest for 10-15 minutes.
Shred the pork with two forks and stir in the pork stock.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the pork onto a tortilla, followed by a slice of avocado, a sprinkle of chopped cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice.
